availableFor: Expert comment on housing, climate and urban policy

recentCoverage.title: What Will Leeds Look Like in 2030?
Coronavirus: We're in a Real-Time Laboratory of a More Sustainable Urban Future
Mutual Home Ownership: the LILAC model

contactOrg: School of Geography, University of Leeds
bio: Paul Chatterton is Professor of Urban Futures at the University of Leeds and one of the UK's leading voices on post-carbon cities, community-led housing, and grassroots climate action. He is the author of How to Save the City (Agenda, 2023) and Unlocking Sustainable Cities (Pluto Press, 2019). Over more than three decades he has worked at the boundary between academic research and real-world change, producing knowledge through action and returning it to the communities who need it most. Paul co-founded LILAC, the UK's first affordable ecological co-housing community, and has led major initiatives including Climate Action Leeds, which engaged over 8,700 people around a shared zero-carbon goal. His work has been featured in the BBC, The Guardian, The Conversation, and numerous international outlets.
contactNote: Paul typically responds to media enquiries within 2 working days.
